Description: AI generates structural metadata (IDs, hooks) before content. Establishes referential integrity.
Implementation Strategy: Use schemas that enforce the generation of regionId, seedId, deadledgerRef, luaModule, etc., as required fields.

Description: Provides predefined structural defaults to guide the AI and ensure a baseline level of correctness.
Implementation Strategy: Define chatDirectorSkeletonProfilev1 mapping archetypeId+objectKind+tier to default envelopes and partial bodies.
Governing Component: plan command embeds or points to a skeleton in the chatDirectorRequestv1 schema

Description: Introduces a dedicated field to indicate the completeness of content.
Implementation Strategy: Add a contentStatus field to schemas with an enum: placeholder, draft, complete.

Description: Replaces informal placeholder text with a controlled set of valid tokens.
Implementation Strategy: Define enum fields for sensitive or optional content, e.g., PLACEHOLDER_EXAMPLE_ONLY, REDACTED_FOR_TIER1, EMPTY.
Governing Component: JSON Schema enum keyword on specific fields (e.g., indices 7, 15)

Description: Restricts placeholders to specific, non-critical fields to preserve core structural integrity.
Implementation Strategy: Design schemas to have separate, clearly named fields for AI placeholders (e.g., placeholderNarrative) vs. human-facing content (e.g., flavorText).
